g.desc: position 1 = +5V <-- DANGER

g.text: position 2 = D22 blinky's LED lives here

g.text: D24 is the THIRD pin in that row and the first pin in the row is +5V.

g.desc: D24 is the THIRD pin in that row and the first pin in the row is +5V.

g.text: Counting from the wrong end, or slipping two positions, lands 5 V on a node

g.desc: Counting from the wrong end, or slipping two positions, lands 5 V on a node

g.text: tied to a 3.3 V I/O pin through 1 kohm - which back-feeds 5 V into PA15 and

g.desc: tied to a 3.3 V I/O pin through 1 kohm - which back-feeds 5 V into PA15 and

g.text: exceeds the SAM3X's absolute maximum input rating of +4.0 V (table 45-1).

g.desc: exceeds the SAM3X's absolute maximum input rating of +4.0 V (table 45-1).

g.text: Count twice. Confirm that position 18 really is ground, with a continuity

g.desc: Count twice. Confirm that position 18 really is ground, with a continuity

g.text: check against a known GND pin, before applying power - and do it with PS3

g.desc: check against a known GND pin, before applying power - and do it with PS3

g.text: disconnected.

g.desc: disconnected.

g.text: The RHS row is numbered the same way and its position 1 is also +5V.

g.desc: The RHS row is numbered the same way and its position 1 is also +5V.

g.text: Nothing on this sheet belongs on the RHS row.

g.desc: Nothing on this sheet belongs on the RHS row.

g.text: WHY THE IRLB8721PbF, AND WHAT "LOGIC LEVEL" ACTUALLY GUARANTEES

g.desc: WHY THE IRLB8721PbF, AND WHAT "LOGIC LEVEL" ACTUALLY GUARANTEES

g.text: VDS 30 V against a 5 V rail

g.desc: VDS 30 V against a 5 V rail

g.text: ID 62 A at VGS = 10 V against a 1 A load

g.desc: ID 62 A at VGS = 10 V against a 1 A load

g.text: VGS(th) 1.35 / 1.80 / 2.35 V min / typ / max

g.desc: VGS(th) 1.35 / 1.80 / 2.35 V min / typ / max

g.text: RDS(on) 8.7 mohm max at VGS = 10 V, ID = 31 A

g.text: (International Rectifier IRLB8721PbF datasheet, 22 April 2009.)

g.desc: (International Rectifier IRLB8721PbF datasheet, 22 April 2009.)

g.text: BE PRECISE ABOUT WHAT IS GUARANTEED. The datasheet's lowest RDS(on) row is at

g.desc: BE PRECISE ABOUT WHAT IS GUARANTEED. The datasheet's lowest RDS(on) row is at

g.text: VGS = 4.5 V. It does not specify RDS(on) at the 3.05 V this gate network

g.desc: VGS = 4.5 V. It does not specify RDS(on) at the 3.05 V this gate network

g.text: delivers, and no honest reading of it claims otherwise. What it does give:

g.desc: delivers, and no honest reading of it claims otherwise. What it does give:

g.text: * VGS(th) max = 2.35 V, so at 3.05 V the part is above threshold by 0.7 V at

g.desc: * VGS(th) max = 2.35 V, so at 3.05 V the part is above threshold by 0.7 V at

g.text: the worst corner. It conducts, guaranteed.

g.desc: the worst corner. It conducts, guaranteed.

g.text: * Figure 3, transfer characteristics, reads roughly 10 A at VGS = 3.0 V -

g.desc: * Figure 3, transfer characteristics, reads roughly 10 A at VGS = 3.0 V -

g.text: ten times this design's load - and figure 1 includes a VGS = 3.0 V output

g.desc: ten times this design's load - and figure 1 includes a VGS = 3.0 V output

g.text: curve. At 1 A the device is well below the knee of that curve, which puts

g.desc: curve. At 1 A the device is well below the knee of that curve, which puts

g.text: it in its ohmic region rather than anywhere near its linear one.

g.desc: it in its ohmic region rather than anywhere near its linear one.

g.text: * Even at a deliberately pessimistic ten times the 4.5 V figure - 160 mohm -

g.desc: * Even at a deliberately pessimistic ten times the 4.5 V figure - 160 mohm -

g.text: a 1 A load dissipates 0.16 W in a part rated at 65 W. There is no thermal

g.desc: a 1 A load dissipates 0.16 W in a part rated at 65 W. There is no thermal

g.text: problem available here, and no heatsink.

g.desc: problem available here, and no heatsink.

g.text: WHAT A NON-LOGIC-LEVEL PART WOULD DO. An IRF540-class MOSFET specifies RDS(on)

g.desc: WHAT A NON-LOGIC-LEVEL PART WOULD DO. An IRF540-class MOSFET specifies RDS(on)

g.text: at VGS = 10 V and allows VGS(th) up to 4 V. At 3.3 V it may not turn on at all,

g.desc: at VGS = 10 V and allows VGS(th) up to 4 V. At 3.3 V it may not turn on at all,

g.text: and if it partly does it sits in its linear region as a resistor of ohms, not

g.desc: and if it partly does it sits in its linear region as a resistor of ohms, not

g.text: milliohms: 1 A through 2 ohm is 2 W in a TO-220 with no heatsink. The failure

g.desc: milliohms: 1 A through 2 ohm is 2 W in a TO-220 with no heatsink. The failure

g.text: is thermal, and it is not subtle.

g.desc: is thermal, and it is not subtle.

g.text: ALTERNATIVES specified at a lower gate voltage, if you would rather have the

g.desc: ALTERNATIVES specified at a lower gate voltage, if you would rather have the

g.text: guarantee than the argument: AO3400A or IRLML6344, both SOT-23 with RDS(on)

g.desc: guarantee than the argument: AO3400A or IRLML6344, both SOT-23 with RDS(on)

g.text: specified at VGS = 2.5 V, both rated well above 1 A. Both need a breakout board

g.desc: specified at VGS = 2.5 V, both rated well above 1 A. Both need a breakout board

g.text: for breadboard work, which is the only reason they are not the first choice.

g.desc: for breadboard work, which is the only reason they are not the first choice.

g.text: SIZING THE GATE NETWORK - R1 = 1 kohm, R2 = 12 kohm

g.desc: SIZING THE GATE NETWORK - R1 = 1 kohm, R2 = 12 kohm

g.text: R1 and R2 pull in opposite directions and all four constraints are hard:

g.desc: R1 and R2 pull in opposite directions and all four constraints are hard:

g.text: R2 SMALL ENOUGH that the internal pull-up cannot lift the gate to threshold

g.desc: R2 SMALL ENOUGH that the internal pull-up cannot lift the gate to threshold

g.text: at reset -> R2 <= about 13 kohm (left)

g.desc: at reset -> R2 <= about 13 kohm (left)

g.text: R2 LARGE ENOUGH that it does not eat the drive, since R1 and R2 divide it:

g.desc: R2 LARGE ENOUGH that it does not eat the drive, since R1 and R2 divide it:

g.text: R1 LARGE ENOUGH that the instant of the edge - when the gate looks like a

g.desc: R1 LARGE ENOUGH that the instant of the edge - when the gate looks like a

g.text: short - stays inside the pin's current group:

g.desc: short - stays inside the pin's current group:

g.text: Ipeak = 3.3 V / 1 kohm = 3.3 mA

g.desc: Ipeak = 3.3 V / 1 kohm = 3.3 mA

g.text: PA15 is Group 1 in table 45-2 note 2: IOH -15 mA, IOL 9 mA

g.desc: PA15 is Group 1 in table 45-2 note 2: IOH -15 mA, IOL 9 mA

g.text: -> 22 % of the source ceiling, 34 % of the sink ceiling on turn-off

g.desc: -> 22 % of the source ceiling, 34 % of the sink ceiling on turn-off

g.text: R1 SMALL ENOUGH that the edge is not absurdly slow:

g.desc: R1 SMALL ENOUGH that the edge is not absurdly slow:

g.text: tau = (R1 || R2) x Ciss = 923 ohm x 1077 pF = 1.0 us

g.desc: tau = (R1 || R2) x Ciss = 923 ohm x 1077 pF = 1.0 us

g.text: Steady state, light on: PA15 sources 3.3 V / 13 kohm = 254 uA. The pin does no

g.desc: Steady state, light on: PA15 sources 3.3 V / 13 kohm = 254 uA. The pin does no

g.text: work. It holds a voltage on a capacitor.

g.desc: work. It holds a voltage on a capacitor.

g.text: WHY NOT FASTER. A 100 ohm gate resistor would switch in 100 ns and would also

g.desc: WHY NOT FASTER. A 100 ohm gate resistor would switch in 100 ns and would also

g.text: violate IOL - 33 mA against a 9 mA ceiling - and a 1 A edge ten times sharper is

g.desc: violate IOL - 33 mA against a 9 mA ceiling - and a 1 A edge ten times sharper is

g.text: ten times the ground bounce for no benefit. Nothing here switches at kilohertz.

g.desc: ten times the ground bounce for no benefit. Nothing here switches at kilohertz.

g.text: This gate moves when a human flips a switch.

g.desc: This gate moves when a human flips a switch.

g.text: WHY PA15 / D24 AND NOT JUST ANY PIN. Table 45-2 splits the SAM3X's I/O into two

g.desc: WHY PA15 / D24 AND NOT JUST ANY PIN. Table 45-2 splits the SAM3X's I/O into two

g.text: current groups that differ by 5x. PA15 is Group 1. PB26 - blinky's LED pin, the

g.desc: current groups that differ by 5x. PA15 is Group 1. PB26 - blinky's LED pin, the

g.text: obvious thing to copy - is Group 2, where the source ceiling is 3 mA and this

g.desc: obvious thing to copy - is Group 2, where the source ceiling is 3 mA and this

g.text: 3.3 mA edge would be out of spec.

g.desc: 3.3 mA edge would be out of spec.

g.text: DEFAULT-OFF IS A PROPERTY OF THE COPPER - AND THE SAM3X FIGHTS IT

g.desc: DEFAULT-OFF IS A PROPERTY OF THE COPPER - AND THE SAM3X FIGHTS IT

g.text: THE REQUIREMENT. The light must be OFF while the Due is in reset, unprogrammed, held in

g.desc: THE REQUIREMENT. The light must be OFF while the Due is in reset, unprogrammed, held in

g.text: reset, or halted after a fault. Firmware cannot provide that: it is not running in three

g.desc: reset, or halted after a fault. Firmware cannot provide that: it is not running in three

g.text: of those four cases.

g.desc: of those four cases.

g.text: The obvious answer is "add a gate pulldown". That is necessary and it is not sufficient,

g.desc: The obvious answer is "add a gate pulldown". That is necessary and it is not sufficient,

g.text: because of this sentence in the SAM3X datasheet (section 31.5.1, PIO pull-up resistor

g.desc: because of this sentence in the SAM3X datasheet (section 31.5.1, PIO pull-up resistor

g.text: control):

g.desc: control):

g.text: "After reset, all of the pull-ups are enabled, i.e. PIO_PUSR resets at the value 0x0."

g.desc: "After reset, all of the pull-ups are enabled, i.e. PIO_PUSR resets at the value 0x0."

g.text: So out of reset PA15 is not high impedance. It is an input with a 50-150 kohm pull-up to

g.desc: So out of reset PA15 is not high impedance. It is an input with a 50-150 kohm pull-up to

g.text: 3.3 V (table 45-2, RPULLUP) sitting in series with R1 and pulling UP on the gate. R2 has

g.desc: 3.3 V (table 45-2, RPULLUP) sitting in series with R1 and pulling UP on the gate. R2 has

g.text: to win that divider, at the worst corner of both tolerance bands.

g.desc: to win that divider, at the worst corner of both tolerance bands.

g.text: WORST CASE - internal pull-up at its 50 kohm minimum, which is the strongest it can be:

g.desc: WORST CASE - internal pull-up at its 50 kohm minimum, which is the strongest it can be:

g.text: Vgate = 3.3 V x R2 / (RPULLUP + R1 + R2)

g.desc: Vgate = 3.3 V x R2 / (RPULLUP + R1 + R2)

g.text: against the IRLB8721PbF's MINIMUM gate threshold of 1.35 V. Off, with better than 2x of

g.desc: against the IRLB8721PbF's MINIMUM gate threshold of 1.35 V. Off, with better than 2x of

g.text: margin, at every corner.

g.desc: margin, at every corner.

g.text: WHAT R2 = 100 kohm WOULD DO - the value a great many gate-drive examples use:

g.desc: WHAT R2 = 100 kohm WOULD DO - the value a great many gate-drive examples use:

g.text: That is above the 1.35 V minimum threshold and above the 1.80 V typical. An unprogrammed

g.desc: That is above the 1.35 V minimum threshold and above the 1.80 V typical. An unprogrammed

g.text: board would sit there partially enhancing Q1 - the MOSFET in its linear region, dropping most

g.desc: board would sit there partially enhancing Q1 - the MOSFET in its linear region, dropping most

g.text: of the 5 V rail across itself and dissipating on the order of a watt, with the light visibly

g.desc: of the 5 V rail across itself and dissipating on the order of a watt, with the light visibly

g.text: glowing and no code running. The design rule, not the number, is what has to survive into any

g.desc: glowing and no code running. The design rule, not the number, is what has to survive into any

g.text: redesign:

g.desc: redesign:

g.text: Vgate at reset must stay below the MOSFET's MINIMUM VGS(th), with the internal

g.desc: Vgate at reset must stay below the MOSFET's MINIMUM VGS(th), with the internal

g.text: pull-up at its MINIMUM resistance.

g.desc: pull-up at its MINIMUM resistance.

g.text: Firmware should still disable the pull-up early (PIO_PUDR) and drive the pin low. That is a

g.desc: Firmware should still disable the pull-up early (PIO_PUDR) and drive the pin low. That is a

g.text: second line of defence, and it cannot be the first one, because it does not exist until an

g.desc: second line of defence, and it cannot be the first one, because it does not exist until an

g.text: image has been flashed and has started running.

g.desc: image has been flashed and has started running.

g.text: THE LOAD, AND THE FLYBACK DIODE THAT IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT

g.desc: THE LOAD, AND THE FLYBACK DIODE THAT IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT

g.text: LA1 is a 5 V light drawing at most 1 A: an LED module, a short length of

g.desc: LA1 is a 5 V light drawing at most 1 A: an LED module, a short length of

g.text: LED strip, or a filament lamp. It is resistive, or LED-plus-resistor. It

g.desc: LED strip, or a filament lamp. It is resistive, or LED-plus-resistor. It

g.text: has no winding, and that is why there is no flyback diode across it. A

g.desc: has no winding, and that is why there is no flyback diode across it. A

g.text: diode across a resistive load does nothing but sit there.

g.desc: diode across a resistive load does nothing but sit there.

g.text: IF YOU EVER SUBSTITUTE AN INDUCTIVE LOAD - a relay coil, a fan, a motor,

g.desc: IF YOU EVER SUBSTITUTE AN INDUCTIVE LOAD - a relay coil, a fan, a motor,

g.text: a solenoid - you must add one (cathode to +5V_LOAD, anode to Q1's drain)

g.desc: a solenoid - you must add one (cathode to +5V_LOAD, anode to Q1's drain)

g.text: before applying power. Collapsing an inductor's field into a MOSFET that

g.desc: before applying power. Collapsing an inductor's field into a MOSFET that

g.text: has just turned off puts hundreds of volts across a 30 V part. It fails

g.desc: has just turned off puts hundreds of volts across a 30 V part. It fails

g.text: shorted, and a low-side MOSFET that fails shorted leaves the light

g.desc: shorted, and a low-side MOSFET that fails shorted leaves the light

g.text: permanently ON - the exact state the rest of this sheet exists to prevent.

g.desc: permanently ON - the exact state the rest of this sheet exists to prevent.

g.text: LOW-SIDE, ON PURPOSE. Low-side means Q1's gate is referenced to ground,

g.desc: LOW-SIDE, ON PURPOSE. Low-side means Q1's gate is referenced to ground,

g.text: the same ground the Due already sits on, so a 3.3 V pin can drive it

g.desc: the same ground the Due already sits on, so a 3.3 V pin can drive it

g.text: directly. A high-side N-channel switch would need its gate driven above

g.desc: directly. A high-side N-channel switch would need its gate driven above

g.text: the 5 V rail, and therefore a charge pump or a gate-driver IC. The price

g.desc: the 5 V rail, and therefore a charge pump or a gate-driver IC. The price

g.text: of low-side is that the load's negative terminal is not at ground when the

g.desc: of low-side is that the load's negative terminal is not at ground when the

g.text: light is off - fine for a 5 V lamp on its own supply, and not fine for

g.desc: light is off - fine for a 5 V lamp on its own supply, and not fine for

g.text: anything a person can touch.

g.desc: anything a person can touch.

g.text: LOW-SIDE LOAD SWITCH

g.desc: LOW-SIDE LOAD SWITCH
